factors.name: Asia Tech Rout & OpenAI IPO Delay
factors.summary: Nikkei down 3.8%, KOSPI down 5.6-8%, SoftBank plunging ~12% on report OpenAI delays IPO to 2027. US futures followed: ES -0.2%, NQ -0.7%. Dow has heavy tech exposure (MSFT, AAPL via holdings); sentiment shock flows through.

factors.name: US PCE Inflation Data (Hot Read)
factors.summary: Recent PCE beat expectations, lifting Fed rate-hike bets materially. Bloomberg reports USD strengthening on 'expectations for further Federal Reserve rate hikes.' Higher real yields pressure equities, especially dividend/rate-sensitive Dow names.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & FX Headwinds
factors.summary: DXY near 101.50, HKD hits 10-month low, GBP/EUR under pressure. Strong dollar erodes multinational earnings translation for Dow (50% international revenue). Dow sensitivity to FX exceeds SPY.

factors.name: 10Y Yields & Real Rates Rising
factors.summary: Fed hike bets rising on hot PCE; long-end yields under pressure. Rate-sensitive Dow components (GS, JPM, utilities, REITs) struggle; dividend yield advantage erodes as nominal yields rise faster.

factors.name: Oil Price Volatility & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: WTI ~$71.50 after Strait of Hormuz ship attack (Iran suspected). Saudi Aramco resumes loading at Ras Tanura post-4mo halt. Oil bid-ask volatile but Iraq OPEC exit threat could push sub-$50 scenario (Mizuho). CVX-heavy Dow mixed signal.

factors.name: Iron Ore & Commodity Weakness
factors.summary: Iron ore set for 7th consecutive weekly loss (worst since 2022) on weak demand & margin compression. Signals softening in China & global capex; pressures CAT, materials demand outlook.

factors.name: Japan Core-Core CPI Surprise (1.9% vs 1.8% est)
factors.summary: Tokyo core-core CPI hit 1.9% YoY, beating 1.8% forecast; core at 1.6% as expected. BOJ front-loading hike discussion now 'in play' per Daiwa. Global hawkish pivot extends, tightens financial conditions, compresses equity valuations.

factors.name: Venezuela Earthquakes (Humanitarian, Low Market Impact)
factors.summary: Twin 7+ magnitude quakes, 235+ dead, 4,300+ injured. Tragic but not a direct market driver for Dow; minimal trade/supply chain disruption reported.

factors.name: Fed Credibility & Forward Guidance Risk
factors.summary: Goolsbee flagged credibility risk from bad guidance; labour market soft (both hiring & layoffs depressed). Fed in no hurry to move either direction, adding near-term uncertainty. Removes strong directional conviction.

factors.name: Dow Valuation & Earnings Revision Risk
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings released in 24h window; earnings season underway but guidance on cost inflation (energy, labour) will be critical. Tech earnings revision risk elevated after IPO delay shock & Apple price-hike concerns.

screening.narrative: Dow opened Friday in a 'Phase 2' selloff driven by a perfect storm: hot US PCE inflation triggering Fed rate-hike bets, a major Asian tech rout (SoftBank -12%, KOSPI -8%) sparked by OpenAI IPO delay to 2027, and surging dollar strength eroding multinational valuations. Japan's core-core CPI beat (1.9% vs 1.8%) reinforced global hawkish pivot. The 24h decline of -1.28% and 4h decline of -0.17% show the move is already underway, with some stabilization risk near 51,600 but conviction remains weak pending major data (Michigan Sentiment, ISM due today/tomorrow).
catalysts_ahead: Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index (due later today, 6/26)
ISM Manufacturing PMI (likely early next week, 7/1)
US Payroll data (if NFP due in early July)
FOMC meeting (if scheduled for early July)
Fed speakers: Goolsbee already spoke; watch for Warsh, Powell commentary on inflation/rates
Dow component earnings guidance (especially CAT, BA, GS, JPM) on capex, cost pressures, FX headwinds
China PMI/economic data (if released over weekend/early week)
Oil/energy update on Hormuz transit & OPEC coordination risk
screening.risk_warning: If US inflation expectations reverse sharply on soft data (Michigan sentiment beats, services CPI cools) or if Fed chairs walk back rate-hike bets, dollar could weaken and equities rebound. Conversely, if Middle East escalation cuts oil supply or if China's growth data disappoints materially, commodity/Fed pivot could accelerate downside beyond 51,600. Geopolitical tail risk (Hormuz, Venezuela) is low but monitor if shipping premiums spike.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2; avoid chasing shorts into 51,600 support. Wait for retest of 51,700–51,800 or a failed bounce into 52,000 resistance before re-engaging on short side. If Michigan Sentiment or ISM surprises dovish later today, pullback entry into 52,050 for fresh longs becomes viable.

factors.name: Tech Sector Contagion & AI Cost Reality
factors.summary: SoftBank plunged ~12%, South Korea's Kospi down 5.6%, with trading halts triggered. Apple raised MacBook/iPad prices due to surging memory chip costs, signaling demand destruction risk and margin pressure across tech suppliers. This cascades to Dow heavyweights (MSFT, Apple indirect exposure via suppliers).

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations Fading
factors.summary: US PCE inflation data released yesterday came cooler than feared, easing market expectations for aggressive Fed rate hikes. USD/JPY stalling at 162.00, USD/CAD sliding, DXY weakening below 101.50—all suggest reduced hawkish Fed bets support risk assets.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk & Hormuz Disruptions
factors.summary: Cargo ship attacked near Strait of Hormuz, UN halted evacuation plan. Oil supply disruption fears resurfacing despite Iran peace deal; WTI at $70.30 but upside capped. Risk-off sentiment dominating as institutional hedges activate on Middle East escalation.

factors.name: Golden Cross / Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: DXY weaker (~101.40), supporting risk assets and multinational earnings translation. Dow has ~50% international revenue, so dollar weakness is moderately bullish for reported earnings of global industrial/finance names.

factors.name: China Economic Headwinds & Tariff Uncertainty
factors.summary: Shanghai Composite -1.7%, PBOC CNY fixing weaker (6.8166 vs 6.8015 estimate). No fresh PMI/growth catalyst; Trump's tariff policies remain a structural overhang for Dow cyclicals (CAT, BA, GE exposure to China demand).

factors.name: Russell Reconstitution (Today, Structural Event)
factors.summary: Semi-annual FTSE Russell rebalancing underway—massive volume day, high mechanical flows. Could amplify volatility but direction neutral; may exacerbate down-moves if risk-off momentum persists.

factors.name: Earnings & Forward Guidance Pipeline
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings surprises visible in news window. UNH, GS, JPM, HD guidance will be critical in coming days; macro uncertainty (tech cost inflation, geopolitical) likely to suppress guidance optimism.

factors.name: Futures Weakness & Asia Spillover
factors.summary: Dow futures down ~0.13%, S&P 500 futures -0.60%, Nasdaq -1.29% at time of latest futures data (06:56 UTC). Asian contagion (Japan -4%, Korea -5.6%) rolling into US session; negative technical momentum into US cash open.

factors.name: Real Yields & Long-End Treasuries
factors.summary: Gold briefly below 4,000 but stabilizing, suggesting real yields not yet spiking further. 10Y/long end likely range-bound given mixed PCE/macro signals. Dividend payers (Dow utility/REIT exposure) not under acute stress.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Input Costs
factors.summary: WTI steady ~$70.30, Brent ease post-Hormuz spike. Energy not acute headwind but geopolitical tail risk keeps volatility bid. CVX (Dow weight) modestly pressured but not a primary driver of index weakness today.

key_levels.resistance: 52200–52400 (recent consolidation high; Russell rebalancing could pin here)

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught in a classic risk-off unwind: Asian tech collapse (AI capex fears, Apple price hikes signaling demand destruction) is rippling into US futures, compounded by renewed Hormuz geopolitical tension and soft China growth signals. However, cooler PCE inflation is easing near-term Fed hawkishness, which normally supports equities. The conflict between cyclical deterioration (tech, supply-chain stress) and monetary relief (lower rate expectations) is creating chop. Russell reconstitution adds mechanical volatility but not directional clarity. Dow weakness is real but—as a value/dividend/industrial index—less exposed than Nasdaq to AI capex cycle collapse.
catalysts_ahead: University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment (final, due today US session)
FOMC decision & Powell presser (next week)
US Jobs Report / NFP (next 2 weeks)
Dow component earnings (UNH, GS, JPM, HD guidance—week ahead)
China PMI / NBS data (if released)
Strait of Hormuz escalation or peace confirmation (ongoing, high-impact intraday driver)
screening.risk_warning: If Fed pivot turns more dovish (market reprices to 3+ cuts in 2027) or Hormuz de-escalates sharply, equities rally hard and this SELL bias inverts. Conversely, if tech earnings disappoint further or China PMI collapses below 45, downside accelerates. Russell rebalancing could create false breakouts; wait for consolidation before chasing shorts.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (30–90 min into Asia/European session). Avoid chasing shorts immediately; wait for intraday retest of 52000–52100 resistance with continued weakness momentum before entering fresh short. Alternatively, scale in on any bounce to 52200 with tight stops at 52400.

factors.name: Tech sector contagion & AI momentum cooling
factors.summary: BofA warns AI momentum is cooling; Magnificent 7 weakness spreading to broader market. SoftBank plunged on OpenAI IPO delay report; Micron down 5% premarket. Tech selloff extended to Asian markets with Kospi halted thrice this week.

factors.name: Fed hawkish repricing (32bps tightening priced by YE2026)
factors.summary: Fed dot plot surprise last week reset market expectations to one rate hike in 2026 vs consensus zero moves. Higher real yields now pressure dividend-heavy Dow names (UNH, JNJ, utilities). This reversal from dovish expectations is structural headwind.

factors.name: US equity fund outflows (first in 3 months)
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports record tech fund withdrawals as AI trade falters. Broad US equity outflows for first time since March signals conviction is breaking. Risk-off momentum underway.

factors.name: Oil slide below $70 (weakens energy input narrative)
factors.summary: WTI at $69.65, lowest since Feb 27, despite Strait of Hormuz tensions (cargo ship attack near Oman). Suggests global demand concerns outweigh geopolitical risk. Lower energy costs support inflation view but weigh on cyclical sentiment.

factors.name: Dollar strength (peak hawkish repricing, then easing)
factors.summary: USD initially supported by Fed hike bets but now showing first post-FOMC decline (per DBS) as markets reassess. EUR/USD testing 1.1350–1.1400. Dollar strength caps multinational Dow earnings translation (50% international revenue).

factors.name: Earnings positioning: Apogee beat, Nike guidance concern
factors.summary: Apogee beat estimates (+$0.16 EPS) but few Dow heavies reporting today. KeyBanc downgraded Nike to Sector Weight on near-term uncertainty. Selective earnings risk, not broad deterioration yet.

factors.name: China hedge fund warning of AI 'super bubble'
factors.summary: Two leading Chinese hedge managers warn AI boom is unsustainable bubble. Suggests institutional conviction shift globally on valuation excesses, hitting Dow tech components (MSFT, AAPL indirect via rotation).

factors.name: Inflation expectations cooling (ECB survey shows 3.5% vs 4.0% prior)
factors.summary: Eurozone consumer one-year inflation expectations fell sharply to 3.5% in May survey. Reduces pressure for further ECB hikes, supports long-end yields stabilization. Mildly supportive for equities vs continued hawkish shock.

factors.name: Russell Reconstitution (structural today)
factors.summary: Once-a-year mandatory index rebalancing occurring today. Can drive outsized single-stock moves but typically neutral directionally for large-cap Dow. Vol spike risk, not directional bias.

factors.name: Prior bias consistency: SELL 5-6/10 (no confirmation)
factors.summary: Two prior sell reads (06-26 03:32, 07:51) both rated 5-6/10 with no confirmation triggered. Today's weakness is validation of sell thesis but move is already -1.28% in 24h — suggests Phase 2/3, late entry risk.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught in a two-day rotation driven by collapsing AI/tech conviction (OpenAI delay, SoftBank rout, Micron weakness) and a surprise Fed hawkish repricing that raised real yields 32bps by year-end. Large-cap equity funds posted their first outflow in three months, and Chinese hedge fund warnings of an AI bubble are amplifying institutional risk-off. While inflation cooling (ECB survey 3.5% vs 4.0%) provides mild relief, the structural shift from dovish to hawkish Fed, combined with tech sector contagion and broad dollar strength limiting multinational Dow earnings, is keeping selling pressure intact. The index is now -1.28% in 24h, putting any new short entry into Phase 2/3 territory.
catalysts_ahead: University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment (final) — due today US session
June month-end portfolio rebalancing (Credit Agricole notes mild USD buying expected)
Russell Reconstitution execution (structural event happening now)
Next major: CPI, NFP in early July; any upside surprise would re-ignite hawkish repricing
Continued Dow heavyweight earnings flow (UNH, BA, CAT forward guidance critical)
Middle East geopolitical escalation or Strait of Hormuz incident (oil volatility wildcard)
screening.risk_warning: Move is already -1.28% in 24h; entering new shorts here risks buying the dip if tech stabilizes or if inflation data soften faster than expected. Invalidation: break above 52,100 with volume would signal reversal from today's sell thesis. Russell Reconstitution volatility could create false breaks. If Fed speakers this week backtrack on hike expectations, hawkish repricing reverses and Dow bounces hard given dividend yield support.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already underway (Phase 2); wait for retest of 51,950–52,050 resistance before shorting again, or consider nibbling shorts into any intraday bounce. Risk-reward poor at current levels given one-day decline already executed. Prefer entry on failed rally or hold for confirmation dip below 51,650 support.

factors.name: PCE Inflation Data (Fresh Release)
factors.summary: US PCE came in softer than feared, reducing Fed hike bets to 31 bps by year-end (down from prior expectations). Lower nominal rates are initially bullish equities, but paired with AI capex deflation fears, this creates ambiguity.

factors.name: AI Cost Deflation & Tech Margin Pressure
factors.summary: Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon announced price increases due to memory-chip supply constraints and AI infrastructure costs. Market is repricing AI-at-all-costs narrative; companies shifting from tokenmaxxing to efficiency. Dow has heavy tech weighting (MSFT, Apple indirectly); margin compression risk.

factors.name: US Trade Deficit Widened
factors.summary: May goods trade deficit hit -$105.8B vs -$85.0B expected, worst since July 2025. Exports fell $11.8B MoM, imports rose $10.9B. This will force Q2 GDP downgrades and signals demand/export weakness.

factors.name: Fund Flow Reversal & Equity Outflows
factors.summary: First equity fund outflows since March reported. Money is rotating from mega-cap tech into illiquid cyclicals, housing, REITs, and small-cap ahead of midterm elections. Headwind for Dow's tech-heavy concentration.

factors.name: Leverage Surge & Volatility Amplification
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports huge surge in leverage deployed to chase the rally; now amplifying downside moves as positions unwind. High-leverage environment is fragile to sharp reversals.

factors.name: Semiconductor Weakness & Chip Stock Rout
factors.summary: Memory giants Samsung and SK Hynix sold off sharply on Apple price-hike announcement. Dow-sensitive names like Intel face input-cost and demand uncertainty. Chip weakness is contagion risk to industrials.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations (Hawkish Repricing Pause)
factors.summary: Fed dot plot last week projected a rate hike this year, driving hawkish repricing. But latest PCE has moderated hike odds. 10-year yields are lower; this should support equities, but AI/margin fears override.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: WTI fell below $70 on Friday, extending losses despite Strait of Hormuz tensions. Lower energy costs are a mild tailwind for transportation-heavy Dow (CVX is a Dow component, but broader impact mixed).

factors.name: US Dollar Softening
factors.summary: USD weakness observed post-PCE; EUR/USD above 1.1400, USD/JPY near intervention threshold at 162. Weaker dollar is typically bullish for multinational earnings (Dow has ~50% international revenue), but headwind offset by tech/trade concerns.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweights Under Pressure
factors.summary: No recent earnings surprises cited, but MSFT, JPM, GS, BA are all exposed to: AI deflation, rate-yield repricing, trade deficit drag, and geopolitical uncertainty (Iran, Ukraine, Middle East). Boeing won a $3.62B order from China Southern (minor bullish tick), but China demand outlook remains weak.

factors.name: Prior Bias Consistency
factors.summary: Three consecutive SELL reads (6/10, 5/10, 5/10) over past ~7 hours with no confirmation triggers. Current -1.28% 24h decline is light, but aligned with persistent bearish sentiment.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tail Risk
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz attack, Venezuela quake, Ukraine war, Iran nuclear tensions all in headlines. Risk-off tone reinforces equity caution; safe-haven flows into Treasuries and gold, away from cyclicals.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between two competing narratives: lower Fed hike expectations (modest equity tailwind) versus a sharp repricing of AI capex returns, widening trade deficits, fund outflows from mega-cap tech, and amplified leverage-driven volatility. Friday's PCE miss and Apple/Microsoft price-hike announcements have triggered a 'risk-off' shuffle—away from growth/tech-driven plays (where Dow has significant exposure) into yield-sensitive and cyclical trades. The -1.28% 24h decline is modest but aligned with three consecutive SELL signals over the past 7 hours, suggesting conviction is building on the downside despite no hard confirmation breakouts yet.
catalysts_ahead: ISM Manufacturing PMI (Mon/Tue next week) — key read on industrial demand for Caterpillar, Boeing, GE, HON, MMM
Durable goods orders & factory orders (mid-week) — direct input for Dow cyclical guidance
China PMI & activity data — critical for CAT, DE, BA export demand
Upcoming Dow component earnings (UNH, JPM, GS, HD) — margin/guidance revisions on AI, rate, trade headwinds
FOMC minutes (if scheduled) or Fed speaker testimony — rate-path clarity
10-year Treasury yield breaks — yield-sensitive financial & dividend valuations
Strait of Hormuz escalation updates — energy & insurance cost implications
screening.risk_warning: INVALIDATION: If PCE data catalyzes a genuine flight-to-safety rally into Treasuries while simultaneously lifting mega-cap tech on AI efficiency re-rating, Dow could snap back above 52,200. Also: any positive surprise from China economic data, CAT/DE forward guidance, or de-escalation on Iran/Hormuz would flip bias to BUY. Leverage unwinding can cut both ways; a sharp rally on short-covering could quickly reverse the -1.28% decline.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already -1.28% in 24h (Phase 3 extended). DO NOT chase shorts here. Wait for a retest of 51,900–52,000 resistance or a daily close below 51,600 support before adding conviction. Given upcoming high-impact catalysts (ISM, China PMI, earnings) and elevated volatility, consider sitting on sidelines until clearer trend confirmation or a relief-bounce entry point emerges.

factors.name: PCE Inflation (Core & Headline)
factors.summary: Latest PCE data 'broadly met forecasts' per FXStreet (2026-06-26T13:57), reducing immediate Fed hike expectations but NOT delivering a cool surprise. This removes downside relief for equities.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Signaling
factors.summary: Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari (2026-06-26T16:04) stated he expects a rate hike this year as inflation remains elevated at >4%. This hawkish signal contradicts market relief from PCE miss and pressures rate-sensitive Dow components.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Strait of Hormuz)
factors.summary: Trump reported Iran violated ceasefire with drone attacks on Hormuz; UAE issued missile alert (later retracted as 'technical malfunction'). Oil briefly spiked but collapsed below $70 and resumed downtrend. Risk premium fragmenting, adding volatility overhang.

factors.name: Oil Price & Energy Input Costs
factors.summary: WTI crude fell below $69 (2026-06-26T15:13), heading for biggest weekly drop in a month despite Hormuz disruption. Low oil may ease inflation but signals weakening global demand—negative for Dow's cyclicals (CAT, GE, MMM, HON).

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Multinational Translation
factors.summary: USD rally faded post-PCE; GBP rebounded 0.20%, AUD recovered near 0.6900. Weaker dollar typically bullish equities, but Dow's 50%+ international revenue exposure now faces margin compression from prior USD strength if trend reverses or consolidates.

factors.name: AI Capex & Tech Sentiment Shift
factors.summary: Bloomberg (2026-06-26T15:57) noted 'Subtle Shift in AI Zeitgeist' with investor rebalancing away from 'tokenmaxxing' to efficiency. Apple, Microsoft, Amazon all signaling price increases on hardware/GPU costs. Sentiment deterioration in mega-cap tech; Dow correlation weakens but broad risk-off tone emerges.

factors.name: Earnings Optimism & Valuation
factors.summary: MarketWatch (2026-06-26T13:31) warns 'Exuberance over earnings is becoming a problem'; Jeremy Grantham calls US market 'most expensive in American history' (2026-06-26T15:34). Valuation stretched; earnings growth expectations may disappoint.

factors.name: European Index Weakness
factors.summary: DAX down 1.25%, CAC 40 down 0.55%, AEX down 0.65%, FTSE 100 down (sentiment sour on Hormuz and tech selloff). Global risk-off spreading; Dow exposed to contagion via multinational holdings and supply-chain linkages.

factors.name: OpenAI IPO Delay & Financial Services Earnings
factors.summary: OpenAI delays IPO to 2027; Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs shares fell on Friday. Banking/advisory fee expectations weakening; Dow financials (GS, JPM) facing headwind from reduced M&A/IPO activity.

factors.name: Consumer Sentiment Near Record Low
factors.summary: US Consumer Sentiment rose to 49.5 (final June) but remains 'close to record low' per Bloomberg (2026-06-26T14:00). Gasoline relief helps, but underlying demand fragility remains; Dow consumer names (HD, WMT proxy exposure) at risk.

factors.name: Bias History Consistency
factors.summary: Three consecutive SELL5/10 reads (06:51, 10:50, 13:10 on 2026-06-26) with no confirmation triggered. Fresh catalyst (geopolitical spike, Fed hawkishness, tech shift) provides new confirmation entry; bias persistence suggests real pressure.

factors.name: Bond Yield / Real Rates
factors.summary: 10Y Treasury not explicitly mentioned in today's news window. Kashkari's hawkish tone should support nominal yields; but PCE data not hot enough to force major re-pricing. Real rate direction unclear; wait for Treasury market close data.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings & Forward Guidance
factors.summary: No new earnings surprises or guidance cuts mentioned in news. Boeing (positive $3.62B order from China) offset by semiconductor weakness (Micron sinks, ON Semi tanks 20% on Synaptics deal). Mixed by component.

screening.narrative: Dow bias remains SELL after three consecutive bearish reads today. Fresh catalyst: Fed hawkishness (Kashkari rate-hike signal vs. soft PCE data), geopolitical noise (Hormuz attack/alert), and a visible shift in AI/tech sentiment away from growth-at-any-cost positioning. European indices closed weak (DAX -1.25%); oil collapsed despite disruption risk, signaling demand concerns. Banking stocks (GS, MS) took hits on OpenAI IPO delay. Valuation stretched per top strategists; consumer sentiment still near lows. Move is underway in PHASE 2 (partial decline visible over last hours), not fully extended yet.
catalysts_ahead: NFP (Employment) — typically first Friday of month; timing unclear but high-impact for Fed rate expectations
ISM Manufacturing PMI (July data cycle starts early July, may be released mid-July — monitor for cyclical strength/weakness)
Fed speakers (Warsh, other FOMC members) — policy messaging on 2026 rate path
Dow component earnings (Nike, Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, others in Q2 season) — forward guidance critical
China PMI and economic data (if released) — Caterpillar, Boeing, industrial demand signals
Treasury auction outcomes and 10Y yield moves — impact on dividend payers and financials within Dow
screening.risk_warning: Risk to bias: (1) If Fed pivots decisively dovish (Warsh or others signal no hikes; PCE trend accelerates lower), equity risk-off could reverse to relief rally. (2) Middle East escalation could spike oil >$75, raising inflation fears and forcing real yields higher—initially negative for Dow. (3) Strong earnings surprises from Dow heavyweights (MSFT, UNH, JPM, BA, CAT) could rebase sentiment and invalidate valuation pessimism. (4) Dollar strengthens sharply on hawkish Fed surprise—bullish for Dow multinationals' gross margins. Monitor 10Y yield and DXY for reversal signals.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (decline underway over last 4–6 hours post-catalyst). Avoid chasing shorts here; wait for a retest of 43,600–43,800 resistance or a dead-cat bounce to enter fresh shorts. Alternatively, place stops above 44,000 and let Dow trade lower into next support at 43,200 before scaling longs.
